rationale.R1.1: Open-ended 'what do you think?' about a research paper most naturally invokes evaluation of the contribution claim (multi-agent arbitration as the key driver) and gap statement; this dimension is central under the research-quality reading.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact makes several inferential moves — that arbitration accounts for 70% of gains, that span-level adequacy gating is necessary, that the framework generalises without retraining — all of which invite logical consistency scrutiny.
rationale.R1.3: BLEU/COMET deltas and ablation percentages are presented as direct support for causal claims about which components drive improvement; alignment between these numbers and those claims is load-bearing under the research-quality reading.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty (span-level arbitration over role-specialised agents) is peripherally relevant — a 'what do you think?' could touch it — but not unambiguously central given the vague delegation.
